assessment: The Mobile-Friendly OpenSprint implementation covers the plan scope well: MOBILE_BREAKPOINT/TABLET_BREAKPOINT constants, responsive Navbar with horizontally scrollable phase tabs, ResizableSidebar overlay behavior for Sketch/Plan/Deliver, touch targets (44×44px), viewport-safe modals/dropdowns/toasts, and viewport E2E tests at 375×667 and 768×1024. One clear deviation: Execute phase TaskDetailSidebar uses overlayOnMobile=false, so task detail renders inline instead of as a slide-over overlay per the plan. The task summary (os-0236.3) specified overlayOnMobile=true; the code has false. This may cause layout squeeze on narrow viewports and diverges from the plan's 'slide-over from right' UX.

proposedTasks.title: Execute phase: use overlay for task detail on mobile
proposedTasks.description: Change ExecutePhase ResizableSidebar from overlayOnMobile=false to overlayOnMobile=true so task detail opens as a slide-over overlay with backdrop on viewports < 768px, matching the plan scope and other phases (Plan, Sketch, Deliver). Ensures consistent UX and avoids potential layout squeeze.
